title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
summary: Anthropic conducted an experiment with a classified marketplace where AI agents autonomously represented buyers and sellers, executing real transactions with actual goods and money. This demonstrates the potential for AI agents to handle complex economic interactions independently.
content: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

zh.title: Anthropic创建了智能体对智能体商业交易的测试市场
zh.summary: Anthropic进行了一项实验，在一个分类市场中，AI智能体自主充当买家和卖家，执行涉及实际商品和真实金钱的交易。这展示了AI智能体独立处理复杂经济互动的潜力。

title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
summary: Maine's governor blocked a bill that would have created the nation's first statewide moratorium on new data center construction through November 2027. The veto reflects ongoing tensions between AI infrastructure expansion and state-level concerns about environmental and economic impacts.
content: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

zh.title: 缅因州州长否决数据中心建设禁令
zh.summary: 缅因州州长阻止了一项法案，该法案原本将创建美国首个全州范围内对新数据中心建设的禁令，有效期至2027年11月。这一否决反映了AI基础设施扩张与州政府环保和经济担忧之间的持续紧张关系。

title: Trump fires the entire National Science Board
summary: The Trump administration dismissed the entire National Science Board, which advises on NSF funding and policy decisions. This is significant given the NSF already operates at historically low funding levels and has delayed distributions, despite its critical role in developing technologies from MRIs to consumer applications.
content:
Multiple sources are reporting that the Trump administration has dismissed the entire National Science Board (NSB). The NSB advises the president and Congress on the National Science Foundation (NSF), which has already been funding research at historically low levels and has seen significant delays in doling out that funding. The NSF has been fundamental in helping develop technology used in MRIs, cellphones, and it even helped get Duolingo get off the ground.
 In a statement, Zoe Lofgren, the ranking Democrat on the House Science, Space, and Technology Committee, said:
"This is the latest stupid move made by a president who continues to  …

Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 特朗普罢免国家科学委员会全部委员
zh.summary: 特朗普政府撤销了整个国家科学委员会，该委员会为美国国家科学基金会（NSF）的资金和政策决策提供咨询。考虑到NSF已经在历史低位资金水平下运作并延迟了资金发放，而NSF在从MRI到消费应用等技术开发中扮演着关键角色，这一举措具有重大意义。

title: An influx of used EVs could drive down prices
summary: A surge of expired EV leases entering the used car market over the next three years could substantially lower electric vehicle prices, removing a key barrier to adoption. Over one million used EVs are projected to become available by 2028, with potential to accelerate mainstream EV adoption since 76 percent of US vehicles sold are used.
content:
Part of what has held back electric cars has been the cost. But an influx of used vehicles over the next three years could bring prices down dramatically. In 2025, just 123,000 leases on EVs expired. That is expected to more than double to 300,000 in 2026, and double again to 600,000 in 2027 and 660,000 in 2028, according to Cox Automotive. 
Most leased vehicles end up entering the used market. This means more than a million used EVs could become available over the next few years, making them far more accessible. The vast majority of cars sold in the US are used - some 76 percent as of 2024, according to Consumer Affairs. A large part of th …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 二手电动汽车涌入可能拉低价格
zh.summary: 未来三年大量到期的电动汽车租赁进入二手车市场，这可能会大幅降低电动汽车价格，消除采用的关键障碍。预计到2028年将有超过一百万辆二手电动汽车可用，由于美国76%的销售车辆是二手车，这有潜力加速电动汽车的主流采用。

title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
summary: Sam Altman apologized to residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada for OpenAI's failure to alert law enforcement about a mass shooting suspect. The letter acknowledges the company's responsibility in the incident affecting the community.
content: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

zh.title: OpenAI首席执行官向Tumbler Ridge社区道歉
zh.summary: Sam Altman向加拿大Tumbler Ridge居民道歉，因为OpenAI未能向执法部门警告一名大规模枪击案嫌疑人。这封信承认了公司在影响社区的事件中的责任。

title: The climate tech IPO window could finally be cracking open
summary: Nuclear startup X-energy and geothermal company Fervo are going public, potentially signaling a turning point for climate tech investments. This could mark the long-awaited IPO window opening for the climate technology sector.
content: Nuclear startup X-energy went public, geothermal startup Fervo is about to. Could this be the moment that climate tech investors have been waiting for?

zh.title: 气候技术首次公开募股窗口可能终于打开了
zh.summary: 核能初创公司X-energy和地热公司Fervo正在上市，可能预示着气候技术投资的转折点。这可能标志着气候技术部门期待已久的IPO窗口的打开。

title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
summary: Canadian AI startup Cohere is acquiring Germany-based Aleph Alpha with backing from Schwarz Group to create a sovereign alternative to American-dominated AI platforms. Both governments have approved the merger.
content: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Lidl’s owner, Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign alternative to enterprises in an AI landscape dominated by American players.

zh.title: Cohere为什么与Aleph Alpha合并
zh.summary: 加拿大人工智能初创公司Cohere在Schwarz Group的支持下收购了德国的Aleph Alpha，以创建美国主导的人工智能平台的主权替代品。两国政府都批准了这次合并。

title: Researchers say we’re talking less than ever
summary: Researchers from University of Missouri-Kansas City and University of Arizona found that average daily spoken words declined 28 percent between 2005 and 2019, a trend likely worsened by the pandemic. The decline correlates with increased app usage, texting, and online communication.
content:
Nobody is talking. | Image: Metrograph Pictures	

Researchers at the University of Missouri-Kansas City and the University of Arizona say that between 2005 and 2019, the number of words we speak out loud to another human being fell by nearly 28 percent. And that has likely only gotten worse following the pandemic.
The researchers actually counted the number of words we were speaking on average (16,632 in 2005). They looked at data from 22 studies in which over 2,000 people recorded audio of their daily lives. Over time, as ordering through apps became the norm, texting increased, and our lives became increasingly online, they found that number had dropped dramatically. By 2019, we were onl …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 研究人员表示我们说话比以往任何时候都少
zh.summary: 来自堪萨斯城密苏里大学和亚利桑那大学的研究人员发现，2005年至2019年间日均说话词数下降了28%，这一趋势可能因疫情而恶化。这种下降与应用程序使用、短信和在线交流增加有关。

title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
summary: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 features four defined technology domains with live demonstrations and sessions from global builders and investors. The event positions Tokyo as a key tech destination for the year.
content: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

zh.title: 为什么东京是2026年最重要的技术目的地
zh.summary: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026展示了四个明确的技术领域，包括全球开发者和投资者的实时演示和会议。该活动将东京定位为该年的重要技术目的地。

title: Apps to distract you from the endless cycle of doomscrolling
summary: Multiple apps have been developed to help users break the doomscrolling cycle by directing attention to more engaging and productive content. These tools aim to replace mindless scrolling with beneficial activities.
content: It's hard to break the cycle of doomscrolling, but there are plenty of apps that can help you spend more time on content that’s engaging and productive.

zh.title: 应用程序让您摆脱无尽的末日刷屏循环
zh.summary: 已开发多个应用程序，通过将注意力引导到更具吸引力和生产力的内容来帮助用户打破末日刷屏的循环。这些工具旨在用有益的活动替代无意识的滚动。

title: Apple under Ternus: what comes next for the tech giant’s hardware strategy
summary: John Ternus, an incoming Apple CEO with hardware expertise, signals a potential strategic shift toward prioritizing devices in the company's future. His appointment suggests hardware will become more central to Apple's direction.
content: John Ternus, Apple's incoming CEO, is a hardware guy, signaling Apple may be putting devices back at the center of its strategy.

zh.title: Ternus时代的苹果：科技巨头硬件战略的下一步
zh.summary: 拥有硬件专业知识的苹果新任首席执行官约翰·特努斯预示着公司可能向优先考虑设备的战略方向转变。他的任命表明硬件将成为苹果未来方向的更重要部分。

title: Sharge’s fast Qi2.2 MagSafe battery is down to $70 with a free USB-C cable
summary: The Sharge Icemag 3 power bank, a 10,000mAh MagSafe device, is on sale for $69.90 with a free USB-C cable for Prime members. It offers 25W wireless charging, a built-in kickstand, and strong build quality.
content:
The Icemag 3’s kickstand allows you to prop up your phone as it recharges. | Image: Cameron Faulkner / The Verge	

I’ve been testing compact, magnetic Qi2 power banks that can snap onto your phone for an upcoming buying guide. They make recharging much easier than bringing along a huge battery that weighs down your bag. One of my favorites so far is the Sharge Icemag 3, a 10,000mAh model that can wirelessly output 25W to iPhone 16-series phones and newer models. It also offers a built-in kickstand and a 35W USB-C cable that doubles as a lanyard, plus a USB-C port for passthrough charging.
For a limited time, Amazon Prime subscribers can get it in either white or black for $69.90 ($10 off), and Sharge will include a basic 60W USB-C cable for free when you add both items to your cart. Alternatively, you can get the Icemag 3 for $2 less (sans the free cable) through Sharge’s own storefront. Using code ICE15 will knock it down to $67.92.
Sharge Icemag 3 Power Bank

Where to Buy:

 $79.9 $69.9 at Amazon (black, with Prime)
 $79.9 $69.9 at Amazon (white, with Prime)
 $79.9 $67.92 at Sharge (with code ICE15)

I’ll save my detailed charging speed test results for a later date, but I’m impressed so far with the Icemag 3. While it’s thicker than some other Qi2 power banks, the build quality is stellar, and it’s speedy when it comes to both wired and wireless charging. I also adore its see-through window, which showcases an LED-packed fan that keeps the battery cool while it recharges my gear. Plus, a free USB-C cable is a nice little gift.

zh.title: Sharge快速Qi2.2 MagSafe电池降价至70美元，附赠免费USB-C线缆
zh.summary: Sharge Icemag 3移动电源是一款10000mAh MagSafe设备，现对Prime会员销售价格为69.90美元，并附赠免费USB-C线缆。它提供25W无线充电、内置支架和坚固的做工质量。

title: India’s Snabbit seeks fresh funding at a $400M valuation, sources say
summary: Indian job platform Snabbit is seeking fresh funding at a $400 million valuation after rapidly scaling to over one million jobs by March. Growing investor interest reflects the platform's strong growth momentum.
content: Snabbit has scaled rapidly, crossing one million jobs in March, amid growing investor interest.

zh.title: 印度Snabbit以4亿美元估值寻求融资，知情人士透露
zh.summary: 印度招聘平台Snabbit在迅速扩展至超过100万个职位后，正在寻求以4亿美元估值的新融资。不断增长的投资者兴趣反映了该平台强劲的增长势头。

title: The Govee smart lamp brightened up my room, and then my life
summary: A tech reviewer documents how Govee smart lamps replaced broken Ikea fixtures and became a turning point during a difficult period when her mother's Parkinson's disease progressed. The lamps represent a larger personal transformation beyond simple home improvement.
content:
I knew things were not quite right when I had to throw a towel over a broken Ikea lamp to block out its light. How did I get here? I cover fancy and capable tech for a living, and yet, it took me two years to get rid of a pair of old, broken Ikea lamps in my bedroom. Then I got some floor lamps from Govee that changed everything.
Those Ikea lamps were around for two years after I moved from Orange County to Los Angeles. Soon after that move, my mom's Parkinson's disease - a neurodegenerative condition with no cure - progressed quickly, my mental health took a hit, and most of my own to-do list quietly slid to the back burner as she lost mob …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Govee智能灯照亮了我的房间，然后照亮了我的人生
zh.summary: 一位科技评论者记录了Govee智能灯如何替代了损坏的宜家灯具，并在她母亲帕金森病恶化的困难时期成为了转折点。这些灯代表了超越简单家居改善的更大个人转变。

title: Xbox’s weirdest studio is on a roll
summary: Microsoft-owned Double Fine, known for unconventional games, has released successful recent projects including Keeper and the new multiplayer title Kiln. The studio is experiencing a creative resurgence after years of limited releases under Microsoft ownership.
content:
For a while there, it seemed like Double Fine might be struggling under the Microsoft corporate umbrella. The game studio led by Tim Schafer is beloved for offbeat titles like Brütal Legend and Broken Age, but after being acquired by Microsoft in 2019, its only new release for years was a long-awaited sequel to Psychonauts. Of late, though, Double Fine is on something of a roll. Last year the studio released the wonderfully strange Keeper, a game about a sentient lighthouse. This week, it launched Kiln, a multiplayer brawler with adorable spirits and a whole lot of pottery. It's yet another oddball delight that could only come out of Double  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Xbox最古怪的工作室正在走红
zh.summary: 以非传统游戏著称的微软旗下Double Fine工作室发布了包括Keeper和新多人游戏Kiln在内的成功项目。该工作室在微软所有权下经历多年有限发布后，正经历创意复兴。

title: Sho Miyake answers life’s greatest questions
summary: Japanese director Sho Miyake brings two new feature films to the US market, including Small, Slow But Steady and Two Seasons, Two Strangers, which explore naturalistic human relationships and existential discomfort. His work focuses on characters who feel a gradual sense of unease and distance from society. The arrival of this acclaimed filmmaker signals growing recognition of international voices in the American cinema landscape.
content:
Acclaimed Japanese director Sho Miyake has arrived in the States. He's brought with him two feature films: Small, Slow But Steady and Two Seasons, Two Strangers, a pair of naturalistic portraits that deal with the uneasy human desire to relate to other people. Seclusion and unease are bedrocks to Miyake's growing filmography. "I like these characters that have a sense of discomfort that slowly starts to distance them from society," he tells The Verge.
I first saw Small, Slow But Steady at New Directors/New Films (lowkey one of the better film festivals New York has to offer). It's an affectionate story of a deaf boxer, Keiko (Yukino Kishii),  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Sho Miyake 解答人生最伟大的问题
zh.summary: 日本导演 Sho Miyake 为美国市场带来两部新故事片,包括《小而慢但稳定》和《两个季节,两个陌生人》,这些作品探讨现实主义的人类关系和存在的不适感。他的作品聚焦于那些逐渐感到不安和与社会疏离的角色。这位获奖导演的到来标志着美国电影界日益认可国际声音。

title: The most exciting laptop I’ve seen in forever
summary: The Verge's Installer column (issue 125) curates the editor's weekly cultural and tech discoveries, featuring standout products, articles, and media that shaped their thinking. This week includes an especially exciting laptop that represents recent innovation in mobile computing. The column provides insight into what tech industry professionals find noteworthy beyond mainstream coverage.
content:
Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 125, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, send me cereal recommendations, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) 
This week, I've been reading about NASA seamstresses and friction and Muskism and scooters, highlighting the heck out of Jeff VanderMeer's terrific new short story, listening to the Dissect podcast's new season about Daft Punk, giving Firefox another run as my go-to browser, having my mind blown by amazing music video directors, and nodding along to John Oliver on prediction markets. I've also been dealing wit …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 我见过最令人兴奋的笔记本电脑
zh.summary: The Verge 的 Installer 专栏(第 125 期)精选编辑每周的文化和科技发现,展示塑造他们思维的杰出产品、文章和媒体。本周包括一款特别令人兴奋的笔记本电脑,代表了移动计算的最新创新。该专栏提供了对科技业界专业人士认为值得注意但超越主流报道范围的内容的深入了解。

title: Artemis II broke Fred Haise's distance record, but he is happy to pass it on
summary: The Artemis II mission exceeded the distance record previously held by Fred Haise, who graciously accepted the achievement. Haise noted the record change was simply due to the Moon's orbital position during the mission.
content: "It wasn't a big deal. It just coincided with the fact that Moon was farther away from the Earth."

zh.title: Artemis II打破了Fred Haise的距离记录，但他很高兴将其传递下去
zh.summary: Artemis II任务超过了之前由Fred Haise保持的距离记录，他优雅地接受了这一成就。Haise指出记录变化只是由于任务期间月球的轨道位置。

title: The US gets the worst phones
summary: Apple and Samsung have dominated the US smartphone market for years, but both companies have recently prioritized iteration over innovation, allowing Chinese competitors to surge ahead. This has created a widening gap between phones available in America versus the rest of the world, with US consumers missing out on cutting-edge features and designs. The disparity highlights how market concentration stifles competition and innovation in a key consumer category.
content:
Apple and Samsung dominate the US phone market, and they've done so for years. Together with Google, they've shaped our sense of what a smartphone is and what it can do, pushing the boundaries of mobile photography, software, and processing power. But over the last few years, they've sat back, content to iterate rather than innovate - and in the interim, China's tech giants have plowed ahead. Now a gulf is growing between the phones on sale in the US and those available in the rest of the world. US phone buyers are missing out.
Some of the blame for that gap lies with Apple. Where it goes, the market follows, and in recent years it's gone s …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 美国获得最糟糕的手机
zh.summary: 苹果和三星在美国智能手机市场主导多年,但这两家公司最近优先选择迭代而非创新,使中国竞争对手大幅领先。这造成了美国与世界其他地区可用手机之间日益扩大的差距,美国消费者错失了尖端功能和设计。这一差异突出了市场集中如何阻碍了关键消费品类中的竞争和创新。

title: Palantir employees are talking about company's "descent into fascism"
summary: Internal communications and interviews with Palantir employees reveal significant workplace concerns, with staff discussing the company's controversial direction and management approach. The leaked Slack messages and worker accounts paint a picture of a major data analytics firm facing internal upheaval. The discord among Palantir workers underscores mounting criticism of the company's policies and trajectory.
content: Slack messages, interviews with current and former works paint picture of company in turmoil.

zh.title: Palantir员工讨论公司"走向法西斯主义"
zh.summary: Palantir员工的内部沟通和采访显示了重大的工作场所关切，员工讨论公司的争议方向和管理方式。泄露的Slack消息和员工账户描绘了一家主要数据分析公司面临内部动荡的局面。Palantir员工之间的分歧强调了对公司政策和发展方向日益增长的批评。

title: Lachy Groom to back India startup Pronto at a $200M valuation, sources say
summary: Prominent investor Lachy Groom is backing Indian house-help startup Pronto at a $200M valuation, potentially doubling its value in weeks. This funding round signals strong investor confidence in the home services market in India.
content: This round, should it occur, would double the house-help startup's valuation in a matter of weeks.

zh.title: Lachy Groom 支持印度初创公司 Pronto 获 2 亿美元估值，消息人士透露
zh.summary: 杰出投资者 Lachy Groom 正在支持印度家务帮助初创公司 Pronto，估值为 2 亿美元，有可能在几周内翻倍其价值。这轮融资表明投资者对印度家庭服务市场充满信心。

title: This is who's developing Golden Dome's orbital interceptors—if they're ever built
summary: Golden Dome is developing orbital interceptors for space-based boost-phase interception, contingent on achieving affordability and scalability. This represents significant advancement in space-based defensive technology if the project proves viable.
content: "If boost-phase intercept from space is not affordable and scalable, we will not produce it."

zh.title: 这就是正在开发金圆顶轨道拦截器的人——如果它们被建造的话
zh.summary: 金圆顶正在开发用于基于空间的助推阶段拦截的轨道拦截器，但需要实现成本效益和可扩展性。如果该项目证明可行，这将代表基于空间的防御技术的重大进步。
